terms.definition: A three-way partition of labeled data: train for fitting, validation for model selection, test for final held-out evaluation. Canonical definition in this topic.
Any labeled partition excluded from fit() — validation and test are hold-out sets; test is the most strictly locked hold-out for final reporting.
Labeled data used during development to compare models and hyperparameters without touching the locked test set.
Labeled data reserved for a final, rarely repeated generalization estimate after model selection is complete.
Partitioning that preserves class (or target) proportions in train, validation, and test — critical for imbalanced classification.
A test partition evaluated only at sign-off — not used iteratively for hyperparameter tuning, to keep the estimate unbiased.
Train/val/test boundaries follow chronological order so future data never appears in training — standard for forecasting and drift-sensitive domains.
All rows belonging to the same entity (user, patient, session) stay in one partition — prevents leakage across train and hold-out sets.
